body:
---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Hila Shmuel <>
Date: Sat, Jul 23, 2016 at 10:44 PM
Subject: Diffusion maps giffs
To: Hila Shmuel <>
explanation: The code reads a GIF image and decomposes it into its individual frames, computing pairwise differences between frames to create a similarity matrix. It then normalizes this matrix and computes the eigenvectors of the resulting matrix, identifying the principal eigenvector to determine a new order of frames. Finally, it reconstructs the GIF image using this new frame order and displays it in grayscale format, allowing for a visual comparison of the reordered frames versus the original.
name: 2016-07-23_diffusion-maps-giffs
subject: Fwd: Diffusion maps giffs
summary: This project analyzes a GIF image by applying diffusion mapping techniques to reorder its frames based on visual similarity. The reordered frames are then displayed in grayscale.
